channel.title: Monthly summaries on TzJ's Net
channel.description: Recent content in Monthly summaries on TzJ's Net

item.title: Monthly Summary 2026-03
item.description: March 2026 achieved transformative breakthroughs across AI systems, robotics, and research infrastructure through systematic application of evolving constraint methodology. Error Recovery Benchmark completed v5.0 architectural redesign shifting from online physics injection to offline trajectory-based VLA context replay, while CalendarPro eliminated single-intent bottleneck via multi-intent LLM classification. Gadget toolkit evolved from quick-overview to deep-excavation research system supporting arbitrary-scale full-text analysis through chunked LLM merge, integrated with MCP server transformation and rclone sync infrastructure establishing clear git-vs-data boundaries. MIHD spatial transcriptomics proved Foundation Models achieve 100% cross-section retrieval vs 14% for PCA while discovering STAIG’s surprising robustness to gene encoder choice. TokenMonitor completed cross-platform migration with polished float ball UI, and ccplan skill introduced 10-phase requirement engineering with adversarial review catching 12+ critical issues pre-implementation. Month exposed recurring patterns of dependency version fragility, documentation drift, and LLM JSON instability while demonstrating 4x speedup via wave-based parallel agent execution and 81% demo reduction through Recovery Behavior Grouping taxonomy.

item.title: Monthly Summary 2026-02
item.description: Intensive research month achieving breakthrough performance improvements in spatial transcriptomics (MIHD) and revealing fundamental limitations in robotic error recovery systems. MIHD project advanced through systematic debugging (coordinate bugs, checkpoint loading, hyperparameter misalignment) and infrastructure optimization (100-500x speedups, embedding caching), achieving valid research-grade metrics (ARI 0.17+). Simultaneously, Error Recovery benchmark scaled from 30 to 2000+ scenes but uncovered catastrophic VLA failure (0% success rates despite fine-tuning), exposing severe distribution shift problems requiring architectural rethinking. Month demonstrated that reproducibility requires holistic alignment across code, hyperparameters, environment, and downstream tools—not just correct logic.
